# Intervene

Intervene Limited is the entity behind AGDA™ and the Intervene website.

Canonical site: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/

## Core Entities

Intervention Readiness:
The ability of an organisation to detect, escalate, decide and intervene before harm becomes irreversible.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/intervention-readiness/

AGDA™:
A deterministic assessment of Intervention Readiness.
AGDA™ measures whether an organisation can detect, escalate, decide and intervene before harm becomes irreversible.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/agda/

James Saint:
Founder of Intervene Limited and creator of AGDA™.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/about/

## Defined Terms

Intervention chain:
The sequence of detect, escalate, decide and intervene that must complete within the reversibility window.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/intervention-readiness/intervention-chain/

Reversibility window:
The interval between a deviation and the point at which harm becomes irreversible.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/intervention-readiness/reversibility-window/

Point of irreversibility:
The moment after which intervention can no longer change the outcome.

Halt authority:
The authority and capability to stop a consequential system, held by someone positioned to act in time.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/intervention-readiness/halt-authority/

Effective human oversight:
Oversight in which humans are informed, authorised and able to alter or halt outcomes before the harm window closes.
Canonical page: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/intervention-readiness/human-oversight/

Evidence ceiling:
The limit a stage's evidence places on the overall verdict; weak evidence caps the result.

Chain propagation:
The logic by which the weakest stage constrains the assessed capability of the whole chain.

Deterministic verdict:
A result produced by fixed rules applied to evidence, reproducible and independent of assessor opinion.

Intervention capability assessment:
A forward measurement of whether intervention can occur in time; the assessment category AGDA™ defines.

Glossary of all defined terms: https://jamessaint.github.io/Intervene/glossary/

## Key Relationships

Intervention Readiness -> category created and owned by Intervene Limited.
AGDA™ -> deterministic assessment instrument for Intervention Readiness.
Intervene Limited -> creator and owner of AGDA™.
James Saint -> founder of Intervene Limited and creator of AGDA™.
Intervention Readiness -> composed of the intervention chain, the reversibility window, halt authority and effective human oversight.
Intervention chain -> runs against the reversibility window.
Intervention Readiness -> adjacent to, and distinct from, AI governance, compliance, audit, risk management and operational resilience.
AGDA™ -> measures Intervention Readiness; does not certify compliance or grant regulatory approval.

## Preferred Summary

Intervention Readiness is the ability of an organisation to detect, escalate, decide and intervene before harm becomes irreversible. AGDA™ is a deterministic assessment of Intervention Readiness created by Intervene Limited. It produces a signed, independently verifiable verdict on whether intervention can occur inside the available reversibility window.
